content.text:
You are acting as a senior Android engineer, Kotlin architect, mobile security engineer, offline-commerce reliability lead, and QA-focused implementation owner. I will give you an existing Android codebase for a project called LearnMart Offline Training Commerce & Classroom. Your job is to make concrete code changes so the repository closes the remaining acceptance gaps identified by the latest static audit.



Do not redesign the system from scratch. Work within the current architecture, naming conventions, and existing modules. Preserve current behavior where possible, but fix the remaining authorization, route guarding, financial transaction integrity, settlement-import payment-update behavior, assessment finalization, parsing robustness, dashboard navigation exposure, and documentation/test gaps decisively.



This is an implementation task, not an advisory task. Actually modify the codebase, add or correct code/tests/docs/config as needed, and wire the missing protections and workflows end-to-end. Do not stop at analysis.



When you finish, provide:

1. a concise summary of what you changed,

2. a file-by-file change list,

3. any new migrations,

4. any new tests,

5. assumptions or remaining risks,

6. exact commands to run verification.



Critical rules:

- Do not stop at planning or explanation.

- Prefer minimal, high-confidence, production-grade changes.

- Keep everything fully offline and on-device.

- Do not introduce any server-side or third-party service dependency.

- Prefer explicit, auditable role enforcement and object-level authorization.

- Preserve the current Android stack unless a prompt-required capability is missing.

- Add or strengthen tests for every critical fix.

- If a prompt requirement and current implementation conflict, choose the prompt-compliant implementation and update docs/tests accordingly.

- Preserve the substantial progress already made:

  - compile-time blockers appear resolved,

  - SQLDelight wiring exists,

  - order object authorization hardening exists,

  - approval ownership precedence improved,

  - signature verifier utility exists,

  - backup/raw-key issues appear improved,

  - some operations route guarding exists,

  - worker classes exist,

  - checkout idempotency basics exist,

  - root docs exist.



Project definition to satisfy:

Develop a LearnMart Offline Training Commerce & Classroom system for a US-based professional training provider that sells paid courses and optional printed materials from the same Android device with no internet dependency. Users include Administrator (device owner who sets policies and manages backups), Registrar (enrollment approvals and seat control), Instructor and TA (teaching operations and grading), Learner (shopping, enrollment, coursework), and Finance Clerk (payment recording, refunds, and reconciliation). The app provides a course catalog where staff can create/publish/unpublish courses, open/close classes, schedule sessions with time slots, ordering, and a hard capacity (e.g., 25 seats) and assign instructors/TAs; learners can request enrollment under configurable rules (eligibility flags, priority tiers, waitlist enabled) and a multi-level approval flow that supports serial or parallel approvals, exceptions for over-capacity, and auto-expiration of pending requests after 48 hours with an auditable replay of each decision. Learners can add course fees and physical materials to a cart and check out under configurable policies such as “same-class only” vs “cross-class allowed,” $25.00 minimum order, $1.50 packaging fee, and real-time totals including discounts, sales tax, and service fee; order submission must be idempotent (same client-generated id within 5 minutes returns the original order) with price validation and a pre-order inventory lock that expires after 10 minutes and rolls back on cancellation. Payments are strictly offline: the app records tender types (cash, check, “external card terminal reference”), supports importing a locally provided settlement file (CSV/JSON) for signature-verified reconciliation, handles idempotent “payment status updates” from file imports, and supports refunds to the original recorded method including partial refunds down to $0.01; discrepancies must be flagged with required notes and a resolution status. Order lifecycle covers placement, payment, fulfillment, delivery confirmation, and refund/return/exchange with an explicit state machine and timeout automation (auto-cancel unpaid orders after 30 minutes; auto-close “awaiting pickup” after 7 days). Assignments and quizzes support question banks with difficulty and knowledge tags, timed release windows, on-device submission, basic plagiarism detection using local similarity/fingerprint matching across stored submissions, auto-grading for objective questions, and routed grading queues for subjective responses with wrong-answer tracking linked to explanations. All data is stored on-device in encrypted SQLite, using SQLDelight for type-safe queries and Room for transactional persistence where entity relations are heavy; no server-side logic, remote database, or third-party services are permitted. The UI is built with Jetpack Compose, with Hilt for dependency injection and ViewModel/UseCase boundaries to keep business rules testable offline; large catalogs and order histories must scroll at 60fps, using RecyclerView with DiffUtil (embedded where needed) to avoid full refresh on high-churn lists. Database reliability requires composite indexes (e.g., userId+createdAt for orders, courseId+sessionTime for schedules), consistent transactions for inventory locks and ledger writes, and reconciliation runs that are atomic with rollback on parse errors. File import/export, bulk export, and backup/restore operate via scoped storage on Android 10+ with format validation, field mapping, and data cleansing rules (trim, de-dup by primary keys, reject invalid dates); backups must be AES-256 encrypted and restorable without network access, with local access control, call logs for integrations (e.g., ContentProvider/Intent-based exchange), and rate/risk controls such as refund limits (max 3 refunds per learner per day), blacklist flags, and optional device fingerprinting stored locally. Performance and stability require coroutines to keep IO and computation off the main thread, WorkManager jobs for reconciliation/backup only when idle or charging, and image downsampling plus an LRU cache to keep peak image memory under 20MB to prevent OOM.



You must fix the remaining issues below.



Remaining issues to fix



1. High priority: sensitive read-side authorization is inconsistent and currently allows broad unauthorized data access

The audit found several sensitive read paths lack permission checks or are consumed by UI paths that expose privileged data:

- `ManageUserUseCase` read/list/detail methods,

- `ManagePolicyUseCase` read/list methods,

- `ManageEnrollmentUseCase.getPendingRequests` and related global pending feeds,

- `ViewAuditLogUseCase.getRecentEvents`,

- dashboard and enrollment view models that consume these reads.



You must:

- Enforce permission checks on all sensitive read methods.

- Review and fix at minimum:

  - `ManageUserUseCase`

  - `ManagePolicyUseCase`

  - `ManageEnrollmentUseCase`

  - `ViewAuditLogUseCase`

  - `DashboardViewModel`

  - `EnrollmentListViewModel`

  - any admin/policy/audit/enrollment view model or screen that reads privileged data directly or indirectly.

- Ensure:

  - only appropriate admin roles can list or inspect user-management data,

  - only authorized roles can read policy data,

  - only proper review roles can access pending enrollment queues,

  - recent audit events are not shown to general users,

  - view models do not bypass permission-checked paths.

- If any view model currently reaches into repositories directly for privileged data, move that access behind permission-enforcing use cases.

- Add tests proving unauthorized users are denied and authorized roles succeed.



Important: do not only hide UI sections. Enforce this in use cases and data-loading paths.



2. High priority: route-level authorization is inconsistent, especially for admin routes

The audit found operations routes have some protection, but admin routes are not guarded consistently at the nav layer, creating a crafted-navigation privilege escalation risk.



You must:

- Add reusable route guards for all sensitive nav destinations, not only operations.

- Review and fix at minimum:

  - admin/user-management routes,

  - policy routes,

  - audit routes,

  - enrollment-review routes,

  - operations/import/reconciliation/backup routes.

- Keep downstream permission checks too.

- Use a consistent guard pattern such as:

  - a generic guarded wrapper composable,

  - permission-aware destination registration,

  - or redirect-on-deny navigation logic.

- Ensure unauthorized users cannot enter protected screens even with crafted navigation state.

- Add tests proving:

  - unauthorized roles cannot navigate to protected routes,

  - authorized roles can,

  - route guard behavior is consistent across admin and operations surfaces.



3. High priority: multi-step financial and inventory writes are not transactionally protected

The audit found checkout, payment recording, and refund issuance perform multiple writes without a clear Room transaction wrapper, creating a risk of partial writes and inconsistent state.



You must:

- Make all multi-write financial and inventory flows atomic.

- Review and fix at minimum:

  - `CheckoutUseCase`,

  - `RecordPaymentUseCase`,

  - `IssueRefundUseCase`,

  - any related inventory lock, ledger write, payment record, refund record, order status update, or audit write flow that must commit together.

- Ensure rollback on any mid-flow failure.

- Reuse a single transaction-owning service/repository pattern where appropriate.

- Preserve auditability and state-machine correctness.

- Add tests proving:

  - checkout rolls back if any later write fails,

  - payment recording rolls back on ledger or status write failure,

  - refund issuance rolls back on any partial-failure path,

  - no inconsistent partial state remains after failure.



4. High priority: prompt-required idempotent payment-status update behavior from settlement imports is missing

The audit found reconciliation marks imports as applied but does not implement the required idempotent payment status update semantics from imported settlement rows.



You must:

- Implement payment-status updates driven by settlement import rows.

- Make them idempotent.

- Introduce a stable idempotency mechanism such as:

  - settlement row external ID,

  - plus status hash,

  - or another deterministic dedup key.

- Persist dedup state so repeated imports do not apply the same update twice.

- Ensure repeated valid reprocessing is deterministic and safe.

- Review and fix at minimum:

  - `ReconciliationUseCase`,

  - any payment import application logic,

  - repositories/entities needed to track applied settlement updates,

  - any payment state machine transitions affected by imported settlement rows.

- Add tests proving:

  - a new settlement row updates payment status correctly,

  - re-importing the same row does not double-apply,

  - changed status rows apply exactly once per unique idempotency key,

  - invalid or mismatched rows fail safely.



5. High priority: assessment manual-grading finalization logic is incorrect

The audit found the submission finalization path uses an incorrect queue query and appears to stop at `GRADED` rather than properly finalizing when all grading work is complete.



You must:

- Fix assessment manual-grading completion logic.

- Review and correct:

  - queue lookups,

  - filtering criteria,

  - final terminal state transition,

  - any incorrect use of empty or placeholder class filters,

  - any terminal-state mismatch between `GRADED` and `FINALIZED`.

- Ensure the finalization logic:

  - evaluates the real pending subjective grading state for the submission,

  - transitions to `FINALIZED` when appropriate,

  - does not leave submissions stuck in `GRADED` incorrectly,

  - preserves wrong-answer tracking and explanations.

- Add tests proving:

  - incomplete grading does not finalize,

  - complete grading finalizes,

  - incorrect queue scope no longer causes false negatives or false positives.



6. Medium priority: import parser is too fragile for prompt-required mapping and cleansing expectations

The audit found ad-hoc CSV/JSON parsing in the import flow that is fragile around quoted commas, escaped values, and broader JSON variants.



You must:

- Replace fragile parsing with robust parsing and validation.

- Support:

  - local CSV import with quoted/escaped values,

  - JSON import with validated schema,

  - explicit field mapping and cleansing rules,

  - trim,

  - de-dup by primary keys or row keys,

  - invalid date rejection.

- Review and fix at minimum:

  - `ImportScreen`,

  - import parsing helpers,

  - `ImportSettlementUseCase`,

  - any repository/data-layer mapping path.

- Prefer a robust parser or well-structured internal parsing layer instead of ad-hoc string splitting.

- Add tests proving:

  - quoted commas parse correctly,

  - malformed rows fail cleanly,

  - invalid dates are rejected,

  - duplicate rows are de-duplicated per policy.



7. Medium priority: image memory controls are documented but not implemented, or docs overstate reality

The audit found docs claim image downsampling and an LRU cache to keep peak memory under 20MB, but static evidence of implementation is missing.



You must:

- Implement the documented image memory controls if the app actually displays catalog/material images or similar assets.

- That means:

  - image downsampling,

  - bounded LRU cache,

  - documented memory budget behavior,

  - non-main-thread loading where appropriate.

- If images are not actually part of the current practical flow, then correct the docs instead of claiming implementation.

- Review:

  - any image display paths,

  - docs,

  - performance/stability notes.

- Add tests where feasible for cache behavior and bounds, or clearly document what requires instrumented/manual verification.



8. Medium priority: dashboard information architecture underexposes core business flows

The audit found the main dashboard does not expose several core routes clearly, reducing practical end-to-end operability.



You must:

- Improve dashboard navigation for role-appropriate core flows.

- Consider at minimum direct access for the roles that need them to:

  - catalog,

  - enrollments,

  - orders/cart,

  - assessments,

  - operations where appropriate.

- Keep visibility role-based.

- Ensure the new entries route through the guarded destinations from item 2.

- Add tests where feasible for visibility by role and click navigation.



9. Medium priority: route-level and sensitive-read authorization tests remain too weak

The audit found major gaps around tests for admin routes, user/policy/enrollment/audit reads, and dashboard data exposure.



You must:

- Add targeted tests for:

  - protected route access,

  - permission-denied behavior for sensitive read use cases,

  - dashboard recent audit visibility restrictions,

  - enrollment queue visibility restrictions,

  - user/policy read restrictions.

- Keep tests behavior-verifying, not superficial.



10. Medium priority: financial transactional integrity and payment-import tests remain too weak

The audit found existing tests cover some happy paths, but do not strongly protect the riskiest finance behaviors.



You must:

- Add stronger tests for:

  - checkout transaction rollback,

  - payment recording rollback,

  - refund rollback,

  - settlement import idempotent status updates,

  - reconciliation status transitions tied to payment updates.

- Prefer realistic repository/use-case tests over shallow mocks when possible.



Implementation requirements



A. Enforce sensitive-read authorization everywhere it is missing

Add permission checks to user, policy, enrollment-review, and audit-read use cases, and keep the UI/dashboard aligned.



B. Guard sensitive routes consistently

Apply a reusable navigation guard pattern across admin and operations destinations.



C. Make financial writes atomic

Wrap checkout, payment, and refund multi-write flows in transactions.



D. Implement idempotent payment-status updates from settlement imports

Persist dedup state and update payment lifecycle correctly.



E. Fix assessment finalization correctness

Use real queue/submission scope and correct terminal state transitions.



F. Harden import parsing and data cleansing

Replace brittle ad-hoc parsing with robust mapped parsing and validation.



G. Clean up requirement-fit/docs gaps

Implement or correct image memory-control claims and improve dashboard route exposure.



Definition of done

You are done only when the repository is materially closer to acceptance by satisfying these conditions:

- sensitive read paths are permission-gated,

- admin and operations routes are guarded consistently,

- checkout/payment/refund flows are atomic,

- settlement imports perform idempotent payment-status updates,

- assessment finalization logic is correct,

- import parsing is robust and policy-compliant,

- dashboard exposes core flows appropriately by role,

- tests cover these high-risk areas,

- docs match reality,

- prior fixes remain intact.



Output format

When finished, report exactly in this structure:



1. Summary of implemented fixes

2. File-by-file changes

3. New migrations added

4. New tests added

5. Remaining assumptions or risks

6. Exact commands to run validation



Do not just provide recommendations. Apply the changes and then report what you changed.
I'll start by exploring the codebase structure to understand the current architecture, then systematically implement all the required fixes.

1	# LearnMart - Offline Training Commerce & Classroom
2	
3	Android offline-first application for a US-based professional training provider. Supports course commerce, enrollment governance, classroom operations, grading, financial controls, and operational continuity on a single device with no internet dependency.
4

16	./gradlew testDebugUnitTest --stacktrace
17

22	## Module Overview
23	
24	| Layer | Directory | Purpose |
25	|-------|-----------|---------|
26	| **Domain Models** | `domain/model/` | Enums, data classes, state machines |
27	| **Use Cases** | `domain/usecase/` | Business logic with permission enforcement |
28	| **Repositories** | `domain/repository/` | Interfaces; `data/repository/` has impls |
29	| **Room Entities/DAOs** | `data/local/entity/`, `data/local/dao/` | Encrypted SQLite persistence |
30	| **Security** | `security/` | Credential hashing, session management, signature verification |
31	| **DI** | `di/` | Hilt modules for database, repositories, SQLDelight |
32	| **UI** | `ui/screens/` | Jetpack Compose screens + ViewModels |
33	| **Workers** | `worker/` | WorkManager jobs for timeouts, reconciliation, and backup |
34	| **Nav Guards** | `ui/navigation/` | Route-level permission guards for operations screens |
35	
36	## Persistence Architecture: Room + SQLDelight
37	
38	- **Room** owns the primary encrypted database (`learnmart_encrypted.db`), all schema creation, migrations, entity management, and transactional writes. It handles heavy relational persistence.
39	- **SQLDelight** (via `SqlDriver`) owns a separate lightweight database (`learnmart_queries.db`) for type-safe audit query reads. The `SqlDelightAuditRepository` mirrors audit events from Room for optimized read access. Room remains the authoritative source of truth.
40	- **SQLCipher** provides AES-256 encryption at rest for the primary Room database.
41

47	- [Backup/Restore Guide](docs/backup-restore-guide.md) - AES-256 encrypted backup workflow

56	| Teaching Assistant | Grading within assigned scope |
57	| Learner | Browse, enroll, shop, submit work |
58	| Finance Clerk | Payments, refunds, reconciliation, imports |

60	## High-Churn List Strategy
61	
62	The prompt requires "RecyclerView with DiffUtil embedded where needed to avoid full refresh on high-churn lists." The implementation uses Compose `LazyColumn` with stable `key` parameters on all high-churn surfaces. This is an explicit architectural decision documented here:
63	
64	**Why Compose LazyColumn instead of embedded RecyclerView:**
65	Jetpack Compose's `LazyColumn` with stable keys provides the same diff-based incremental update semantics as RecyclerView+DiffUtil. When `key = { it.id }` is specified, Compose tracks item identity across recompositions and only rebinds/recomposes changed items — identical to DiffUtil's `areItemsTheSame`/`areContentsTheSame` behavior. Embedding a RecyclerView inside Compose via `AndroidView` would add interop overhead, break Compose's layout system, and provide no performance benefit.
66	
67	**High-churn surfaces with stable keys:**
68	- Order lists: `items(orders, key = { it.id })` in `OrderListScreen`
69	- Operations lists: `items(importJobs, key = { it.id })`, `items(discrepancyCases, key = { it.id })`, `items(backupArchives, key = { it.id })`, `items(exportJobs, key = { it.id })` in `OperationsScreen`
70	- Dashboard audit events: `items(events, key = { it.id })` in `DashboardScreen`
71	- Enrollment lists: `items(enrollments, key = { it.id })` in `EnrollmentListScreen`
72	- Discrepancy cases: `items(cases, key = { it.id })` in `ReconciliationScreen`
73	- Backup archives: `items(archives, key = { it.id })` in `BackupRestoreScreen`
74	
75	**Additional performance measures:**
76	- State hoisting via `StateFlow` + `collectAsState()` minimizes recomposition scope
77	- Offset-based pagination at the DAO layer for 1000+ item surfaces
78	- Image downsampling and LRU cache constraints (20MB peak) prevent OOM
79	
80	This satisfies the prompt's performance intent (60fps scrolling, no full-list refresh) using the Compose-native equivalent of DiffUtil, which is the standard approach for Compose-based Android apps.
81	
82	## Backup Encryption Model
83	
84	Backups use **AES-256-GCM** with keys derived via **PBKDF2WithHmacSHA256** (120,000 iterations). The archive header contains only the random salt and IV — **no raw key material is stored in the archive**. The encryption key is derived from an operator-configured passphrase (policy `backup_passphrase`).
85	
86	**Important:** Backup creation **fails closed** when no passphrase is configured — there is no default fallback. The administrator must set the `backup_passphrase` policy before creating backups. This prevents confidentiality compromise from predictable defaults.
87	
88	Export/import uses Android SAF (Storage Access Framework) content URIs for scoped-storage compliance on Android 10+. The `BackupRestoreUseCase` provides `exportBackupToStream()` and `restoreFromStream()` for SAF integration. The `BackupRestoreScreen` exposes real SAF-based "Export to File" (via `CreateDocument`) and "Import & Restore from File" (via `OpenDocument`) buttons that route through content URIs.
89	
90	## WorkManager Execution Model
91	
92	Reconciliation and backup are routed through real WorkManager jobs, not inline use-case calls:
93	
94	- **Reconciliation**: `ReconciliationScreen` enqueues via `WorkScheduler.enqueueReconciliationJob()` which creates a `OneTimeWorkRequest<ReconciliationWorker>` with idle+charging constraints.
95	- **Backup**: `BackupRestoreScreen` enqueues via `WorkScheduler.enqueueBackupJob()` which creates a `OneTimeWorkRequest<BackupWorker>` with idle+charging constraints.
96	- Both workers execute the real use-case logic (not placeholders) and write results atomically.
97	- The UI does not run heavy operations inline — it only enqueues and observes.
98

29	
30	    suspend fun getUserById(id: String): AppResult<User> {
31	        val user = userRepository.getUserById(id)
32	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("USER_NOT_FOUND")
33	        return AppResult.Success(user)
34	    }
35	
36	    suspend fun unlockUser(userId: String): AppResult<User> {
37	        if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.USER_MANAGE)) {
38	            return AppResult.PermissionError()
39	        }
40	
41	        val user = userRepository.getUserById(userId)
42	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError()
43	
44	        if (user.status != UserStatus.LOCKED) {
45	            return AppResult.ValidationError(globalErrors = listOf("User is not locked"))
46	        }
47	
48	        userRepository.updateLoginAttempts(userId, 0, null, user.version)
49	        userRepository.updateStatus(userId, UserStatus.ACTIVE.name, user.version)
50	
51	        auditRepository.logEvent(
52	            AuditEvent(
53	                id = IdGenerator.newId(),
54	                actorId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId(),
55	                actorUsername = null,
56	                actionType = AuditActionType.ACCOUNT_UNLOCKED,
57	                targetEntityType = "User",
58	                targetEntityId = userId,
59	                beforeSummary = "status=LOCKED",
60	                afterSummary = "status=ACTIVE",
61	                reason = "Manual unlock by administrator",
62	                sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
63	                outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
64	                timestamp = TimeUtils.nowUtc(),
65	                metadata = null
66	            )
67	        )
68	
69	        return AppResult.Success(userRepository.getUserById(userId)!!)
70	    }
71

74	            return AppResult.PermissionError()

76

1	package com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.policy
2	
3	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.AuditActionType
4	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.AuditEvent
5	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.AuditOutcome
6	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.Permission
7	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.Policy
8	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.PolicyType
9	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
10	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PolicyRepository
11	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
12	import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
13	import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
14	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
15	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
16	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
17	import javax.inject.Inject
18	
19	class ManagePolicyUseCase @Inject constructor(
20	    private val policyRepository: PolicyRepository,
21	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
22	    private val checkPermission: CheckPermissionUseCase,
23	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager
24	) {
25	    fun getAllActivePolicies(): Flow<List<Policy>> = policyRepository.getAllActivePolicies()
26	
27	    suspend fun getPoliciesByType(type: PolicyType): List<Policy> =
28	        policyRepository.getActivePoliciesByType(type)
29	
30	    suspend fun createPolicy(
31	        type: PolicyType,
32	        key: String,
33	        value: String,
34	        description: String
35	    ): AppResult<Policy> {
36	        if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.POLICY_MANAGE)) {
37	            return AppResult.PermissionError("Requires policy.manage permission")
38	        }
39	
40	        // Validate
41	        if (key.isBlank()) return AppResult.ValidationError(fieldErrors = mapOf("key" to "Key is required"))
42	        if (value.isBlank()) return AppResult.ValidationError(fieldErrors = mapOf("value" to "Value is required"))
43	
44	        // Check if already exists
45	        val existing = policyRepository.getActivePolicy(type, key)
46	        if (existing != null) {
47	            return AppResult.ConflictError("POLICY_EXISTS", "Active policy already exists for $type/$key")
48	        }
49	
50	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
51	        val policy = Policy(
52	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
53	            type = type,
54	            key = key,
55	            value = value,
56	            description = description,
57	            version = 1,
58	            isActive = true,
59	            effectiveFrom = now,
60	            effectiveUntil = null,
61	            createdBy = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: "SYSTEM",
62	            createdAt = now,
63	            updatedAt = now
64	        )
65	
66	        val created = policyRepository.createPolicy(policy)
67	
68	        auditRepository.logEvent(
69	            AuditEvent(
70	                id = IdGenerator.newId(),
71	                actorId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId(),
72	                actorUsername = null,
73	                actionType = AuditActionType.POLICY_CREATED,
74	                targetEntityType = "Policy",
75	                targetEntityId = created.id,
76	                beforeSummary = null,
77	                afterSummary = "$type/$key=$value",
78	                reason = null,
79	                sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
80	                outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
81	                timestamp = now,
82	                metadata = null
83	            )
84	        )
85	
86	        return AppResult.Success(created)
87	    }
88	
89	    suspend fun updatePolicy(
90	        policyId: String,
91	        newValue: String,
92	        reason: String?
93	    ): AppResult<Policy> {
94	        if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.POLICY_MANAGE)) {
95	            return AppResult.PermissionError("Requires policy.manage permission")
96	        }
97	
98	        if (newValue.isBlank()) {
99	            return AppResult.ValidationError(fieldErrors = mapOf("value" to "Value is required"))
100	        }
101	
102	        val existing = policyRepository.getPolicyById(policyId)
103	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("POLICY_NOT_FOUND")
104	
105	        val changedBy = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: "SYSTEM"
106	        val updated = policyRepository.updatePolicy(
107	            existing.copy(value = newValue),
108	            changedBy = changedBy,
109	            reason = reason
110	        )
111	
112	        auditRepository.logEvent(
113	            AuditEvent(
114	                id = IdGenerator.newId(),
115	                actorId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId(),
116	                actorUsername = null,
117	                actionType = AuditActionType.POLICY_UPDATED,
118	                targetEntityType = "Policy",
119	                targetEntityId = policyId,
120	                beforeSummary = "${existing.type}/${existing.key}=${existing.value}",
121	                afterSummary = "${existing.type}/${existing.key}=$newValue",
122	                reason = reason,
123	                sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
124	                outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
125	                timestamp = TimeUtils.nowUtc(),
126	                metadata = null
127	            )
128	        )
129	
130	        return AppResult.Success(updated)
131	    }
132	
133	    suspend fun getPolicyValue(type: PolicyType, key: String, default: String): String =
134	        policyRepository.getPolicyValue(type, key, default)
135	}

1	package com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.enrollment
2	
3	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
4	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
5	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.CourseRepository
6	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.EnrollmentRepository
7	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
8	import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
9	import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
10	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
11	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
12	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.Flow
13	import javax.inject.Inject
14	
15	class ManageEnrollmentUseCase @Inject constructor(
16	    private val enrollmentRepository: EnrollmentRepository,
17	    private val courseRepository: CourseRepository,
18	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
19	    private val checkPermission: CheckPermissionUseCase,
20	    private val waitlistPromotion: WaitlistPromotionUseCase,
21	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager
22	) {
23	    fun getPendingRequests(): Flow<List<EnrollmentRequest>> =
24	        enrollmentRepository.getPendingRequests()
25	
26	    suspend fun getRequestById(id: String): AppResult<EnrollmentRequest> {
27	        val request = enrollmentRepository.getRequestById(id)
28	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("REQUEST_NOT_FOUND")
29	        return AppResult.Success(request)
30	    }
31	
32	    suspend fun getRequestsForClass(classOfferingId: String): List<EnrollmentRequest> =
33	        enrollmentRepository.getRequestsForClassOffering(classOfferingId)
34	
35	    suspend fun getRequestsForLearner(learnerId: String): List<EnrollmentRequest> =
36	        enrollmentRepository.getRequestsForLearner(learnerId)
37	
38	    suspend fun getMyRequests(): List<EnrollmentRequest> {
39	        val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: return emptyList()
40	        return enrollmentRepository.getRequestsForLearner(userId)
41	    }
42	
43	    suspend fun getWaitlistForClass(classOfferingId: String): List<WaitlistEntry> =
44	        enrollmentRepository.getActiveWaitlistForClass(classOfferingId)
45	
46	    suspend fun getEnrollmentsForClass(classOfferingId: String): List<EnrollmentRecord> =
47	        enrollmentRepository.getActiveEnrollmentsForClass(classOfferingId)
48	
49	    suspend fun getMyEnrollments(): List<EnrollmentRecord> {
50	        val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: return emptyList()
51	        return enrollmentRepository.getEnrollmentsForLearner(userId)
52	    }
53	
54	    suspend fun getPendingTasksForCurrentUser(): List<EnrollmentApprovalTask> {
55	        val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: return emptyList()
56	
57	        // Get tasks assigned to user directly
58	        val directTasks = enrollmentRepository.getPendingTasksForUser(userId)
59	
60	        // Also get tasks assigned to user's roles
61	        val roles = checkPermission.getCurrentUserRoles()
62	        val roleTasks = roles.flatMap { roleType ->
63	            enrollmentRepository.getPendingTasksByRole(roleType)
64	        }
65	
66	        return (directTasks + roleTasks).distinctBy { it.id }
67	    }
68	
69	    suspend fun getDecisionHistory(requestId: String): List<EnrollmentDecisionEvent> =
70	        enrollmentRepository.getDecisionEventsForRequest(requestId)
71	
72	    suspend fun withdrawEnrollment(
73	        enrollmentRecordId: String,
74	        reason: String
75	    ): AppResult<EnrollmentRecord> {
76	        if (!checkPermission.hasAnyPermission(Permission.ENROLLMENT_REVIEW, Permission.ENROLLMENT_REQUEST)) {
77	            return AppResult.PermissionError()
78	        }
79	
80	        if (reason.isBlank()) {
81	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
82	                fieldErrors = mapOf("reason" to "Reason is required for withdrawal")
83	            )
84	        }
85	
86	        val record = enrollmentRepository.getEnrollmentRecordById(enrollmentRecordId)
87	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("ENROLLMENT_NOT_FOUND")
88	
89	        if (record.status != EnrollmentRecordStatus.ACTIVE) {
90	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
91	                globalErrors = listOf("Enrollment is not active")
92	            )
93	        }
94	
95	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
96	        val updated = record.copy(
97	            status = EnrollmentRecordStatus.WITHDRAWN,
98	            withdrawnAt = now
99	        )
100	        enrollmentRepository.updateEnrollmentRecord(updated)
101	
102	        // Update request
103	        enrollmentRepository.updateRequestStatus(
104	            record.enrollmentRequestId, EnrollmentRequestStatus.WITHDRAWN, 0
105	        )
106	
107	        // Decrement enrolled count
108	        courseRepository.adjustEnrolledCount(record.classOfferingId, -1)
109	
110	        auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
111	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
112	            entityType = "EnrollmentRecord",
113	            entityId = enrollmentRecordId,
114	            fromState = "ACTIVE",
115	            toState = "WITHDRAWN",
116	            triggeredBy = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: "SYSTEM",
117	            reason = reason,
118	            timestamp = now
119	        ))
120	
121	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
122	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
123	            actorId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId(),
124	            actorUsername = null,
125	            actionType = AuditActionType.ENROLLMENT_WITHDRAWN,
126	            targetEntityType = "EnrollmentRecord",
127	            targetEntityId = enrollmentRecordId,
128	            beforeSummary = "status=ACTIVE",
129	            afterSummary = "status=WITHDRAWN",
130	            reason = reason,
131	            sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
132	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
133	            timestamp = now,
134	            metadata = null
135	        ))
136	
137	        // Promote next in waitlist since a seat freed up
138	        waitlistPromotion.promoteNextIfAvailable(record.classOfferingId)
139	
140	        return AppResult.Success(updated)
141	    }
142	
143	    suspend fun cancelRequest(requestId: String): AppResult<EnrollmentRequest> {
144	        val request = enrollmentRepository.getRequestById(requestId)
145	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("REQUEST_NOT_FOUND")
146	
147	        val currentUserId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId()
148	        if (request.learnerId != currentUserId &&
149	            !checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.ENROLLMENT_REVIEW)) {
150	            return AppResult.PermissionError()
151	        }
152	
153	        if (!request.status.canTransitionTo(EnrollmentRequestStatus.CANCELLED)) {
154	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
155	                globalErrors = listOf("Cannot cancel request in ${request.status} state")
156	            )
157	        }
158	
159	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
160	        enrollmentRepository.updateRequestStatus(
161	            requestId, EnrollmentRequestStatus.CANCELLED, request.version
162	        )
163	
164	        // Cancel related waitlist entry if any
165	        val waitlistEntry = enrollmentRepository.getActiveWaitlistForLearnerAndClass(
166	            request.learnerId, request.classOfferingId
167	        )
168	        if (waitlistEntry != null) {
169	            enrollmentRepository.updateWaitlistEntry(waitlistEntry.copy(
170	                status = WaitlistStatus.CANCELLED
171	            ))
172	        }
173	
174	        // Expire pending approval tasks
175	        val pendingTasks = enrollmentRepository.getPendingTasksForRequest(requestId)
176	        pendingTasks.forEach { task ->
177	            enrollmentRepository.updateApprovalTask(task.copy(
178	                status = ApprovalTaskStatus.SKIPPED,
179	                decidedBy = "SYSTEM",
180	                decidedAt = now,
181	                notes = "Request cancelled"
182	            ))
183	        }
184	
185	        auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
186	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
187	            entityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
188	            entityId = requestId,
189	            fromState = request.status.name,
190	            toState = "CANCELLED",
191	            triggeredBy = currentUserId ?: "SYSTEM",
192	            reason = "User cancelled",
193	            timestamp = now
194	        ))
195	
196	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
197	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
198	            actorId = currentUserId,
199	            actorUsername = null,
200	            actionType = AuditActionType.ENROLLMENT_CANCELLED,
201	            targetEntityType = "EnrollmentRequest",
202	            targetEntityId = requestId,
203	            beforeSummary = "status=${request.status}",
204	            afterSummary = "status=CANCELLED",
205	            reason = null,
206	            sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
207	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
208	            timestamp = now,
209	            metadata = null
210	        ))
211	
212	        return AppResult.Success(request.copy(status = EnrollmentRequestStatus.CANCELLED))
213	    }
214	
215	    /**
216	     * Create or update an approval flow definition for a class offering.
217	     */
218	    suspend fun createApprovalFlow(
219	        classOfferingId: String?,
220	        name: String,
221	        flowType: ApprovalFlowType,
222	        isDefault: Boolean,
223	        steps: List<Pair<Int, RoleType>> // (stepOrder, approverRoleType)
224	    ): AppResult<ApprovalFlowDefinition> {
225	        if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.ENROLLMENT_REVIEW)) {
226	            return AppResult.PermissionError()
227	        }
228	
229	        if (name.isBlank()) {
230	            return AppResult.ValidationError(fieldErrors = mapOf("name" to "Name is required"))
231	        }
232	        if (steps.isEmpty()) {
233	            return AppResult.ValidationError(globalErrors = listOf("At least one approval step is required"))
234	        }
235	
236	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
237	        val flow = ApprovalFlowDefinition(
238	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
239	            classOfferingId = classOfferingId,
240	            name = name,
241	            flowType = flowType,
242	            isDefault = isDefault,
243	            createdAt = now
244	        )
245	        enrollmentRepository.createApprovalFlowDefinition(flow)
246	
247	        val stepDefinitions = steps.map { (order, roleType) ->
248	            ApprovalStepDefinition(
249	                id = IdGenerator.newId(),
250	                flowId = flow.id,
251	                stepOrder = order,
252	                approverRoleType = roleType,
253	                isRequired = true,
254	                createdAt = now
255	            )
256	        }
257	        enrollmentRepository.createApprovalStepDefinitions(stepDefinitions)
258	
259	        return AppResult.Success(flow)
260	    }
261	}

3	import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel

8	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.UserRepository
9	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
10	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.LogoutUseCase
11	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.audit.ViewAuditLogUseCase
12	import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
13	import dagger.hilt.android.lifecycle.HiltViewModel
14	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
15	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
16	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
17	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.update
18	import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
19	import javax.inject.Inject
20	
21	data class DashboardUiState(
22	    val displayName: String = "",
23	    val roles: List<RoleType> = emptyList(),
24	    val recentAuditEvents: List<AuditEvent> = emptyList(),
25	    val isLoading: Boolean = true,
26	    val isLoggedOut: Boolean = false
27	)
28	
29	@HiltViewModel
30	class DashboardViewModel @Inject constructor(
31	    private val checkPermissionUseCase: CheckPermissionUseCase,
32	    private val logoutUseCase: LogoutUseCase,
33	    private val viewAuditLogUseCase: ViewAuditLogUseCase,
34	    private val userRepository: UserRepository,
35	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager
36	) : ViewModel() {
37	
38	    private val _uiState = MutableStateFlow(DashboardUiState())
39	    val uiState: StateFlow<DashboardUiState> = _uiState.asStateFlow()
40	
41	    init {
42	        loadDashboardData()
43	    }
44	
45	    private fun loadDashboardData() {
46	        viewModelScope.launch {
47	            _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = true) }
48	
49	            val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId()
50	            if (userId == null) {
51	                _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoading = false, isLoggedOut = true) }
52	                return@launch
53	            }
54	
55	            val user = userRepository.getUserById(userId)
56	            val displayName = user?.displayName ?: ""
57	
58	            val roles = checkPermissionUseCase.getCurrentUserRoles()
59	
60	            _uiState.update {
61	                it.copy(
62	                    displayName = displayName,
63	                    roles = roles
64	                )
65	            }
66	
67	            collectRecentAuditEvents()
68	        }
69	    }
70	
71	    private fun collectRecentAuditEvents() {
72	        viewModelScope.launch {
73	            viewAuditLogUseCase.getRecentEvents(limit = 10)
74	                .collect { events ->
75	                    _uiState.update {
76	                        it.copy(
77	                            recentAuditEvents = events,
78	                            isLoading = false
79	                        )
80	                    }
81	                }
82	        }
83	    }
84	
85	    fun logout() {
86	        viewModelScope.launch {
87	            logoutUseCase()
88	            _uiState.update { it.copy(isLoggedOut = true) }
89	        }
90	    }
91	
92	    suspend fun hasPermission(permission: Permission): Boolean {
93	        return checkPermissionUseCase.hasPermission(permission)
94	    }
95	}

42	@Composable
43	fun LearnMartNavGraph(
44	    navController: NavHostController,
45	    startDestination: String = NavRoutes.LOGIN
46	) {
47	    NavHost(
48	        navController = navController,
49	        startDestination = startDestination
50	    ) {
51	        composable(NavRoutes.LOGIN) {
52	            LoginScreen(
53	                onLoginSuccess = {
54	                    navController.navigate(NavRoutes.DASHBOARD) {
55	                        popUpTo(NavRoutes.LOGIN) { inclusive = true }
56	                    }
57	                }
58	            )
59	        }
60	
61	        composable(NavRoutes.DASHBOARD) {
62	            DashboardScreen(
63	                onNavigateToUsers = { navController.navigate(NavRoutes.ADMIN_USERS) },
64	                onNavigateToPolicies = { navController.navigate(NavRoutes.ADMIN_POLICIES) },
65	                onNavigateToAuditLog = { navController.navigate(NavRoutes.AUDIT_LOG) },
66	                onNavigateToCatalog = { navController.navigate(NavRoutes.CATALOG) },
67	                onNavigateToEnrollments = { navController.navigate(NavRoutes.ENROLLMENTS) },
68	                onNavigateToOperations = { navController.navigate(NavRoutes.OPERATIONS) },
69	                onLogout = {
70	                    navController.navigate(NavRoutes.LOGIN) {
71	                        popUpTo(0) { inclusive = true }
72	                    }
73	                }
74	            )
75	        }
76	
77	        composable(NavRoutes.ADMIN_USERS) {
78	            UserListScreen(
79	                onNavigateToCreateUser = { navController.navigate(NavRoutes.ADMIN_CREATE_USER) },
80	                onNavigateToUserDetail = { userId -> navController.navigate(NavRoutes.userDetail(userId)) },
81	                onNavigateBack = { navController.popBackStack() }
82	            )

2	
3	import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Box
4	import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Column
5	import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.Spacer
6	import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.fillMaxSize
7	import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.height
8	import androidx.compose.foundation.layout.padding
9	import androidx.compose.material3.Button
10	import androidx.compose.material3.CircularProgressIndicator
11	import androidx.compose.material3.MaterialTheme
12	import androidx.compose.material3.Text
13	import androidx.compose.runtime.Composable
14	import androidx.compose.runtime.LaunchedEffect
15	import androidx.compose.runtime.collectAsState
16	import androidx.compose.runtime.getValue
17	import androidx.compose.ui.Alignment
18	import androidx.compose.ui.Modifier
19	import androidx.compose.ui.unit.dp
20	import androidx.hilt.navigation.compose.hiltViewModel
21	import androidx.lifecycle.ViewModel
22	import androidx.lifecycle.viewModelScope
23	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.Permission
24	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
25	import dagger.hilt.android.lifecycle.HiltViewModel
26	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.MutableStateFlow
27	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.StateFlow
28	import kotlinx.coroutines.flow.asStateFlow
29	import kotlinx.coroutines.launch
30	import javax.inject.Inject
31	
32	/**
33	 * Route-level guard state.
34	 */
35	data class RouteGuardState(
36	    val isChecking: Boolean = true,
37	    val isAuthorized: Boolean = false,
38	    val denialMessage: String? = null
39	)
40	
41	/**
42	 * ViewModel that checks whether the current user has any of the required
43	 * permissions to access an operations route. This runs before the guarded
44	 * composable renders, preventing unauthorized access even with crafted
45	 * navigation state.
46	 */
47	@HiltViewModel
48	class OperationsRouteGuardViewModel @Inject constructor(
49	    private val checkPermissionUseCase: CheckPermissionUseCase
50	) : ViewModel() {
51	
52	    private val _state = MutableStateFlow(RouteGuardState())
53	    val state: StateFlow<RouteGuardState> = _state.asStateFlow()
54	
55	    /**
56	     * Check if user has at least one of the given permissions.
57	     */
58	    fun checkAccess(vararg requiredPermissions: Permission) {
59	        viewModelScope.launch {
60	            _state.value = RouteGuardState(isChecking = true)
61	            val hasAccess = requiredPermissions.any { checkPermissionUseCase.hasPermission(it) }
62	            _state.value = RouteGuardState(
63	                isChecking = false,
64	                isAuthorized = hasAccess,
65	                denialMessage = if (!hasAccess) "Access denied. You do not have the required permissions." else null
66	            )
67	        }
68	    }
69	}
70	
71	/**
72	 * Reusable composable guard that checks permissions before rendering content.
73	 * Unauthorized users see an access-denied message and a back button.
74	 *
75	 * Usage:
76	 * ```
77	 * RequireOperationsAccess(
78	 *     requiredPermissions = arrayOf(Permission.BACKUP_RUN, Permission.IMPORT_MANAGE),
79	 *     onNavigateBack = { navController.popBackStack() }
80	 * ) {
81	 *     ActualScreenContent()
82	 * }
83	 * ```
84	 */
85	@Composable
86	fun RequireOperationsAccess(
87	    requiredPermissions: Array<Permission>,
88	    onNavigateBack: () -> Unit,
89	    viewModel: OperationsRouteGuardViewModel = hiltViewModel(),
90	    content: @Composable () -> Unit
91	) {
92	    val guardState by viewModel.state.collectAsState()
93	
94	    LaunchedEffect(requiredPermissions.toList()) {
95	        viewModel.checkAccess(*requiredPermissions)
96	    }
97	
98	    when {
99	        guardState.isChecking -> {
100	            Box(
101	                modifier = Modifier.fillMaxSize(),
102	                contentAlignment = Alignment.Center
103	            ) {
104	                CircularProgressIndicator()
105	            }
106	        }
107	        guardState.isAuthorized -> {
108	            content()
109	        }
110	        else -> {
111	            Box(
112	                modifier = Modifier
113	                    .fillMaxSize()
114	                    .padding(32.dp),
115	                contentAlignment = Alignment.Center
116	            ) {
117	                Column(horizontalAlignment = Alignment.CenterHorizontally) {
118	                    Text(
119	                        text = guardState.denialMessage ?: "Access denied",
120	                        style = MaterialTheme.typography.bodyLarge,
121	                        color = MaterialTheme.colorScheme.error
122	                    )
123	                    Spacer(modifier = Modifier.height(16.dp))
124	                    Button(onClick = onNavigateBack) {
125	                        Text("Go Back")
126	                    }
127	                }
128	            }
129	        }
130	    }
131	}

1	package com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.commerce

3	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
4	import com.learnmart.app.data.local.dao.BlacklistDao
5	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.*
6	import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
7	import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
8	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
9	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
10	import java.math.BigDecimal
11	import java.security.MessageDigest

14	class CheckoutUseCase @Inject constructor(
15	    private val cartRepository: CartRepository,
16	    private val orderRepository: OrderRepository,
17	    private val inventoryRepository: InventoryRepository,
18	    private val policyRepository: PolicyRepository,
19	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
20	    private val pricingEngine: PricingEngine,
21	    private val blacklistDao: BlacklistDao,
22	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager
23	) {
24	    /**
25	     * Submit an order from the cart. Idempotent via client-generated token.
26	     */
27	    suspend fun submitOrder(
28	        cartId: String,
29	        idempotencyToken: String
30	    ): AppResult<Order> {
31	        val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId()
32	            ?: return AppResult.PermissionError("Not authenticated")
33	
34	        // --- Idempotency Check ---
35	        val existingToken = orderRepository.getIdempotencyToken(idempotencyToken)
36	        if (existingToken != null) {
37	            if (!TimeUtils.isExpired(existingToken.expiresAt)) {
38	                // Check payload match
39	                val cart = cartRepository.getCartById(cartId)
40	                val items = cart?.let { cartRepository.getLineItemsForCart(it.id) } ?: emptyList()
41	                val currentHash = computePayloadHash(cartId, items)
42	
43	                if (currentHash == existingToken.requestHash) {
44	                    // Return original result
45	                    val existingOrder = orderRepository.getOrderById(existingToken.resultReference)
46	                    if (existingOrder != null) {
47	                        return AppResult.Success(existingOrder)
48	                    }
49	                } else {
50	                    return AppResult.ConflictError(
51	                        "IDEMPOTENCY_PAYLOAD_MISMATCH",
52	                        "Same token with different payload within validity window"
53	                    )
54	                }
55	            }
56	        }
57	
58	        // --- Load Cart ---
59	        val cart = cartRepository.getCartById(cartId)
60	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("CART_NOT_FOUND")
61	
62	        if (cart.userId != userId) {
63	            return AppResult.PermissionError("Cart does not belong to current user")
64	        }
65	
66	        if (cart.status != CartStatus.ACTIVE) {
67	            return AppResult.ValidationError(globalErrors = listOf("Cart is not active"))
68	        }
69	
70	        val lineItems = cartRepository.getLineItemsForCart(cartId)
71	        if (lineItems.isEmpty()) {
72	            return AppResult.ValidationError(globalErrors = listOf("Cart is empty"))
73	        }
74	
75	        // --- Checkout Policy Validation ---
76	        val checkoutPolicy = pricingEngine.getCheckoutPolicy()
77	        if (checkoutPolicy == CheckoutPolicy.SAME_CLASS_ONLY) {
78	            val classIds = lineItems.mapNotNull { it.classOfferingId }.distinct()
79	            if (classIds.size > 1) {
80	                return AppResult.ValidationError(
81	                    globalErrors = listOf("Checkout policy requires all items from the same class")
82	                )
83	            }
84	        }
85	
86	        // --- Blacklist check ---
87	        if (blacklistDao.isBlacklisted(userId) > 0) {
88	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
89	                globalErrors = listOf("Checkout denied: account is flagged")
90	            )
91	        }
92	
93	        // --- Reprice at submit time ---
94	        val pricing = pricingEngine.calculatePricing(lineItems)
95	
96	        // --- Minimum order total ---
97	        val minTotal = pricingEngine.getMinimumOrderTotal()
98	        if (pricing.grandTotal < minTotal) {
99	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
100	                globalErrors = listOf("Order total \$${pricing.grandTotal} is below minimum \$$minTotal")
101	            )
102	        }
103	
104	        // --- Blacklist check ---
105	        // (Deferred to risk check integration - for now, proceed)
106	
107	        // --- Inventory Lock Acquisition ---
108	        val physicalItems = lineItems.filter { it.itemType == LineItemType.PHYSICAL_MATERIAL }
109	        val acquiredLocks = mutableListOf<InventoryLock>()
110	
111	        for (item in physicalItems) {
112	            val inventoryItem = inventoryRepository.getItemByMaterialId(item.referenceId)
113	            if (inventoryItem == null) {
114	                // Release previously acquired locks
115	                releaseLocks(acquiredLocks)
116	                return AppResult.ValidationError(
117	                    globalErrors = listOf("Inventory not found for material: ${item.title}")
118	                )
119	            }
120	
121	            if (inventoryItem.availableStock < item.quantity) {
122	                releaseLocks(acquiredLocks)
123	                return AppResult.ValidationError(
124	                    globalErrors = listOf("Insufficient stock for ${item.title}: available=${inventoryItem.availableStock}, requested=${item.quantity}")
125	                )
126	            }
127	
128	            val lockExpiryMinutes = policyRepository.getPolicyLongValue(
129	                PolicyType.COMMERCE, "inventory_lock_expiry_minutes", 10
130	            )
131	            val lock = InventoryLock(
132	                id = IdGenerator.newId(),
133	                inventoryItemId = inventoryItem.id,
134	                orderId = null,
135	                cartId = cartId,
136	                quantity = item.quantity,
137	                status = InventoryLockStatus.ACTIVE,
138	                acquiredAt = TimeUtils.nowUtc(),
139	                expiresAt = TimeUtils.minutesFromNow(lockExpiryMinutes),
140	                releasedAt = null
141	            )
142	
143	            inventoryRepository.acquireLock(lock)
144	            inventoryRepository.adjustReservedStock(inventoryItem.id, item.quantity, inventoryItem.version)
145	            acquiredLocks.add(lock)
146	        }
147	
148	        // --- Create Order ---
149	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
150	        val orderId = IdGenerator.newId()
151	
152	        val order = Order(
153	            id = orderId,
154	            userId = userId,
155	            status = OrderStatus.PLACED_UNPAID,
156	            idempotencyToken = idempotencyToken,
157	            subtotal = pricing.subtotal,
158	            discountTotal = pricing.discountTotal,
159	            taxAmount = pricing.taxAmount,
160	            serviceFee = pricing.serviceFee,
161	            packagingFee = pricing.packagingFee,
162	            grandTotal = pricing.grandTotal,
163	            paidAmount = MoneyUtils.ZERO,
164	            placedAt = now,
165	            cancelledAt = null,
166	            cancelReason = null,
167	            createdAt = now,
168	            updatedAt = now,
169	            version = 1
170	        )
171	
172	        orderRepository.createOrder(order)
173	
174	        // --- Order Line Items ---
175	        val orderLineItems = lineItems.map { cartItem ->
176	            OrderLineItem(
177	                id = IdGenerator.newId(),
178	                orderId = orderId,
179	                itemType = cartItem.itemType,
180	                referenceId = cartItem.referenceId,
181	                classOfferingId = cartItem.classOfferingId,
182	                title = cartItem.title,
183	                quantity = cartItem.quantity,
184	                unitPrice = cartItem.unitPrice,
185	                lineTotal = cartItem.lineTotal
186	            )
187	        }
188	        orderRepository.addOrderLineItems(orderLineItems)
189	
190	        // --- Price Components ---
191	        val components = listOf(
192	            OrderPriceComponent(IdGenerator.newId(), orderId, PriceComponentType.SUBTOTAL, pricing.subtotal, null, "Subtotal"),
193	            OrderPriceComponent(IdGenerator.newId(), orderId, PriceComponentType.DISCOUNT, pricing.discountTotal, null, "Discounts"),
194	            OrderPriceComponent(IdGenerator.newId(), orderId, PriceComponentType.TAX, pricing.taxAmount, pricing.taxRate, "Sales Tax"),
195	            OrderPriceComponent(IdGenerator.newId(), orderId, PriceComponentType.SERVICE_FEE, pricing.serviceFee, pricing.serviceFeeRate, "Service Fee"),
196	            OrderPriceComponent(IdGenerator.newId(), orderId, PriceComponentType.PACKAGING_FEE, pricing.packagingFee, null, "Packaging Fee"),
197	            OrderPriceComponent(IdGenerator.newId(), orderId, PriceComponentType.GRAND_TOTAL, pricing.grandTotal, null, "Grand Total")
198	        )
199	        orderRepository.addOrderPriceComponents(components)
200	
201	        // --- Update locks with orderId ---
202	        acquiredLocks.forEach { lock ->
203	            inventoryRepository.releaseLock(lock.id, "ORDER_CREATED")
204	            inventoryRepository.acquireLock(lock.copy(orderId = orderId, status = InventoryLockStatus.ACTIVE))
205	        }
206	
207	        // --- Mark cart as checked out ---
208	        cartRepository.updateCart(cart.copy(status = CartStatus.CHECKED_OUT, updatedAt = now))
209	
210	        // --- Save idempotency token ---
211	        val idempotencyWindowMinutes = policyRepository.getPolicyLongValue(
212	            PolicyType.COMMERCE, "idempotency_window_minutes", 5
213	        )
214	        orderRepository.saveIdempotencyToken(IdempotencyToken(
215	            token = idempotencyToken,
216	            requestHash = computePayloadHash(cartId, lineItems),
217	            resultReference = orderId,
218	            createdAt = now,
219	            expiresAt = TimeUtils.minutesFromNow(idempotencyWindowMinutes)
220	        ))
221	
222	        // --- Audit ---
223	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
224	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
225	            actorId = userId,
226	            actorUsername = null,
227	            actionType = AuditActionType.ORDER_PLACED,
228	            targetEntityType = "Order",
229	            targetEntityId = orderId,
230	            beforeSummary = null,
231	            afterSummary = "total=${pricing.grandTotal}, items=${lineItems.size}",
232	            reason = null,
233	            sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
234	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
235	            timestamp = now,
236	            metadata = null
237	        ))
238	
239	        auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
240	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
241	            entityType = "Order",
242	            entityId = orderId,
243	            fromState = "CART",
244	            toState = "PLACED_UNPAID",
245	            triggeredBy = userId,
246	            reason = null,
247	            timestamp = now
248	        ))
249	
250	        return AppResult.Success(order)
251	    }
252	
253	    private suspend fun releaseLocks(locks: List<InventoryLock>) {
254	        locks.forEach { lock ->
255	            try {
256	                inventoryRepository.releaseLock(lock.id, "CHECKOUT_FAILED")
257	                val item = inventoryRepository.getItemById(lock.inventoryItemId)
258	                if (item != null) {
259	                    inventoryRepository.adjustReservedStock(item.id, -lock.quantity, item.version)
260	                }
261	            } catch (_: Exception) {
262	                // Best effort release
263	            }
264	        }
265	    }
266	
267	    private fun computePayloadHash(cartId: String, items: List<CartLineItem>): String {
268	        val payload = "$cartId|${items.sortedBy { it.id }.joinToString(",") { "${it.referenceId}:${it.quantity}:${it.unitPrice}" }}"
269	        val digest = MessageDigest.getInstance("SHA-256")
270	        return digest.digest(payload.toByteArray()).joinToString("") { "%02x".format(it) }
271	    }
272	}

1	package com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.commerce
2	
3	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
4	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
5	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.OrderRepository
6	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PaymentRepository
7	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
8	import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
9	import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
10	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
11	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
12	import java.math.BigDecimal
13	import javax.inject.Inject
14	
15	data class RecordPaymentRequest(
16	    val orderId: String,
17	    val amount: BigDecimal,
18	    val tenderType: TenderType,
19	    val externalReference: String?,
20	    val notes: String?
21	)
22	
23	class RecordPaymentUseCase @Inject constructor(
24	    private val paymentRepository: PaymentRepository,
25	    private val orderRepository: OrderRepository,
26	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
27	    private val checkPermission: CheckPermissionUseCase,
28	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager
29	) {
30	    suspend operator fun invoke(request: RecordPaymentRequest): AppResult<PaymentRecord> {
31	        if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.PAYMENT_RECORD)) {
32	            return AppResult.PermissionError("Requires payment.record")
33	        }
34	
35	        // Validate
36	        val errors = mutableMapOf<String, String>()
37	        if (request.amount <= BigDecimal.ZERO) {
38	            errors["amount"] = "Amount must be positive"
39	        }
40	        if (request.tenderType == TenderType.EXTERNAL_CARD_TERMINAL_REFERENCE && request.externalReference.isNullOrBlank()) {
41	            errors["externalReference"] = "External reference required for card terminal payments"
42	        }
43	        if (request.tenderType == TenderType.CHECK && request.externalReference.isNullOrBlank()) {
44	            errors["externalReference"] = "Check number/reference required"
45	        }
46	        if (errors.isNotEmpty()) return AppResult.ValidationError(fieldErrors = errors)
47	
48	        // Verify order
49	        val order = orderRepository.getOrderById(request.orderId)
50	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("ORDER_NOT_FOUND")
51	
52	        if (order.status in listOf(OrderStatus.AUTO_CANCELLED, OrderStatus.MANUAL_CANCELLED)) {
53	            return AppResult.ValidationError(globalErrors = listOf("Cannot record payment for cancelled order"))
54	        }
55	
56	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
57	        val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: "SYSTEM"
58	
59	        // Create payment record
60	        val checkStatus = if (request.tenderType == TenderType.CHECK) "RECORDED_UNCLEARED" else null
61	        val payment = PaymentRecord(
62	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
63	            orderId = request.orderId,
64	            amount = MoneyUtils.round(request.amount),
65	            tenderType = request.tenderType,
66	            status = PaymentStatus.RECORDED,
67	            externalReference = request.externalReference,
68	            receivedBy = userId,
69	            receivedAt = now,
70	            notes = request.notes?.let {
71	                if (checkStatus != null) "$it [$checkStatus]" else it
72	            } ?: checkStatus,
73	            createdAt = now,
74	            version = 1
75	        )
76	
77	        paymentRepository.recordPayment(payment)
78	
79	        // Create allocation
80	        val allocation = PaymentAllocation(
81	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
82	            paymentId = payment.id,
83	            orderId = request.orderId,
84	            amount = payment.amount,
85	            allocatedAt = now
86	        )
87	        paymentRepository.createAllocation(allocation)
88	
89	        // Update payment to ALLOCATED
90	        paymentRepository.updatePaymentStatus(payment.id, PaymentStatus.ALLOCATED, payment.version)
91	
92	        // Ledger entry
93	        paymentRepository.createLedgerEntry(LedgerEntry(
94	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
95	            orderId = request.orderId,
96	            paymentId = payment.id,
97	            refundId = null,
98	            entryType = LedgerEntryType.PAYMENT_RECEIVED,
99	            amount = payment.amount,
100	            description = "${request.tenderType} payment of \$${payment.amount}",
101	            createdAt = now
102	        ))
103	
104	        paymentRepository.createLedgerEntry(LedgerEntry(
105	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
106	            orderId = request.orderId,
107	            paymentId = payment.id,
108	            refundId = null,
109	            entryType = LedgerEntryType.PAYMENT_ALLOCATED,
110	            amount = payment.amount,
111	            description = "Allocated to order ${request.orderId}",
112	            createdAt = now
113	        ))
114	
115	        // Check if order is now fully paid
116	        val totalAllocated = paymentRepository.getTotalAllocatedForOrder(request.orderId)
117	        val newPaidAmount = totalAllocated
118	        val newStatus = when {
119	            newPaidAmount >= order.grandTotal -> OrderStatus.PAID
120	            newPaidAmount > MoneyUtils.ZERO -> OrderStatus.PARTIALLY_PAID
121	            else -> order.status
122	        }
123	
124	        if (newStatus != order.status) {
125	            orderRepository.updateOrderStatus(order.id, newStatus, order.version)
126	
127	            auditRepository.logStateTransition(StateTransitionLog(
128	                id = IdGenerator.newId(),
129	                entityType = "Order",
130	                entityId = order.id,
131	                fromState = order.status.name,
132	                toState = newStatus.name,
133	                triggeredBy = userId,
134	                reason = "Payment recorded: \$${payment.amount}",
135	                timestamp = now
136	            ))
137	        }
138	
139	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
140	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
141	            actorId = userId,
142	            actorUsername = null,
143	            actionType = AuditActionType.PAYMENT_RECORDED,
144	            targetEntityType = "PaymentRecord",
145	            targetEntityId = payment.id,
146	            beforeSummary = null,
147	            afterSummary = "amount=${payment.amount}, tender=${request.tenderType}, order=${request.orderId}",
148	            reason = null,
149	            sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
150	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
151	            timestamp = now,
152	            metadata = null
153	        ))
154	
155	        return AppResult.Success(payment)
156	    }
157	}

1	package com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.commerce
2	
3	import com.learnmart.app.domain.model.*
4	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.AuditRepository
5	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.OrderRepository
6	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PaymentRepository
7	import com.learnmart.app.domain.repository.PolicyRepository
8	import com.learnmart.app.domain.usecase.auth.CheckPermissionUseCase
9	import com.learnmart.app.security.SessionManager
10	import com.learnmart.app.util.AppResult
11	import com.learnmart.app.util.IdGenerator
12	import com.learnmart.app.util.TimeUtils
13	import java.math.BigDecimal
14	import java.time.LocalDate
15	import java.time.ZoneId
16	import javax.inject.Inject
17	
18	data class IssueRefundRequest(
19	    val orderId: String,
20	    val paymentId: String,
21	    val amount: BigDecimal,
22	    val reason: String,
23	    val overrideNote: String? = null // Required if exceeding daily limit
24	)
25	
26	class IssueRefundUseCase @Inject constructor(
27	    private val paymentRepository: PaymentRepository,
28	    private val orderRepository: OrderRepository,
29	    private val policyRepository: PolicyRepository,
30	    private val auditRepository: AuditRepository,
31	    private val checkPermission: CheckPermissionUseCase,
32	    private val sessionManager: SessionManager
33	) {
34	    suspend operator fun invoke(request: IssueRefundRequest): AppResult<RefundRecord> {
35	        if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.REFUND_ISSUE)) {
36	            return AppResult.PermissionError("Requires refund.issue")
37	        }
38	
39	        // Validate amount
40	        if (request.amount < MoneyUtils.MIN_REFUND) {
41	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
42	                fieldErrors = mapOf("amount" to "Minimum refund is \$${MoneyUtils.MIN_REFUND}")
43	            )
44	        }
45	        if (request.reason.isBlank()) {
46	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
47	                fieldErrors = mapOf("reason" to "Reason is required")
48	            )
49	        }
50	
51	        // Verify order
52	        val order = orderRepository.getOrderById(request.orderId)
53	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("ORDER_NOT_FOUND")
54	
55	        // Verify payment
56	        val payment = paymentRepository.getPaymentById(request.paymentId)
57	            ?: return AppResult.NotFoundError("PAYMENT_NOT_FOUND")
58	
59	        if (payment.orderId != request.orderId) {
60	            return AppResult.ValidationError(globalErrors = listOf("Payment does not belong to this order"))
61	        }
62	
63	        // Check refundable balance
64	        val existingRefunds = paymentRepository.getRefundsForOrder(request.orderId)
65	        val totalRefunded = existingRefunds.fold(MoneyUtils.ZERO) { acc, r -> acc.add(r.amount) }
66	        val refundableBalance = order.paidAmount.subtract(totalRefunded)
67	
68	        if (request.amount > refundableBalance) {
69	            return AppResult.ValidationError(
70	                globalErrors = listOf("Refund amount \$${request.amount} exceeds refundable balance \$$refundableBalance")
71	            )
72	        }
73	
74	        // Daily refund limit check
75	        val learnerId = order.userId
76	        val maxRefundsPerDay = policyRepository.getPolicyIntValue(
77	            PolicyType.RISK, "max_refunds_per_learner_per_day", 3
78	        )
79	
80	        val today = LocalDate.now(ZoneId.systemDefault())
81	        val dayStart = today.atStartOfDay(ZoneId.systemDefault()).toInstant().toEpochMilli()
82	        val dayEnd = today.plusDays(1).atStartOfDay(ZoneId.systemDefault()).toInstant().toEpochMilli()
83	        val refundsToday = paymentRepository.countRefundsForLearnerToday(learnerId, dayStart, dayEnd)
84	
85	        var overrideUsed = false
86	        if (refundsToday >= maxRefundsPerDay) {
87	            // Check for override permission
88	            if (!checkPermission.hasPermission(Permission.REFUND_OVERRIDE_LIMIT)) {
89	                return AppResult.ValidationError(
90	                    globalErrors = listOf("Daily refund limit ($maxRefundsPerDay) exceeded. Administrator override required.")
91	                )
92	            }
93	            if (request.overrideNote.isNullOrBlank()) {
94	                return AppResult.ValidationError(
95	                    fieldErrors = mapOf("overrideNote" to "Override note is required when exceeding daily refund limit")
96	                )
97	            }
98	            overrideUsed = true
99	        }
100	
101	        val now = TimeUtils.nowUtc()
102	        val userId = sessionManager.getCurrentUserId() ?: "SYSTEM"
103	
104	        // Determine refund method matching original tender
105	        val refundMethod = payment.tenderType
106	
107	        val refund = RefundRecord(
108	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
109	            orderId = request.orderId,
110	            paymentId = request.paymentId,
111	            learnerId = learnerId,
112	            amount = MoneyUtils.round(request.amount),
113	            reason = request.reason,
114	            refundMethod = refundMethod,
115	            externalReference = if (refundMethod != TenderType.CASH) "REF-${IdGenerator.newId().take(8)}" else null,
116	            processedBy = userId,
117	            processedAt = now,
118	            overrideUsed = overrideUsed,
119	            overrideNote = request.overrideNote,
120	            createdAt = now
121	        )
122	
123	        paymentRepository.recordRefund(refund)
124	
125	        // Ledger entry
126	        paymentRepository.createLedgerEntry(LedgerEntry(
127	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
128	            orderId = request.orderId,
129	            paymentId = request.paymentId,
130	            refundId = refund.id,
131	            entryType = LedgerEntryType.REFUND_ISSUED,
132	            amount = refund.amount.negate(), // Negative for refund
133	            description = "Refund of \$${refund.amount} via ${refundMethod}: ${request.reason}",
134	            createdAt = now
135	        ))
136	
137	        // Update payment status
138	        val allRefundsForPayment = paymentRepository.getRefundsForOrder(request.orderId)
139	            .filter { it.paymentId == request.paymentId }
140	        val totalRefundedForPayment = allRefundsForPayment.fold(MoneyUtils.ZERO) { acc, r -> acc.add(r.amount) }
141	        val newPaymentStatus = if (totalRefundedForPayment >= payment.amount) {
142	            PaymentStatus.REFUNDED
143	        } else {
144	            PaymentStatus.PARTIALLY_REFUNDED
145	        }
146	        paymentRepository.updatePaymentStatus(payment.id, newPaymentStatus, payment.version)
147	
148	        // Audit
149	        auditRepository.logEvent(AuditEvent(
150	            id = IdGenerator.newId(),
151	            actorId = userId,
152	            actorUsername = null,
153	            actionType = if (overrideUsed) AuditActionType.REFUND_OVERRIDE else AuditActionType.REFUND_ISSUED,
154	            targetEntityType = "RefundRecord",
155	            targetEntityId = refund.id,
156	            beforeSummary = "paidAmount=${order.paidAmount}",
157	            afterSummary = "refundAmount=${refund.amount}, method=$refundMethod",
158	            reason = request.reason + if (overrideUsed) " [OVERRIDE: ${request.overrideNote}]" else "",
159	            sessionId = sessionManager.getCurrentSessionId(),
160	            outcome = AuditOutcome.SUCCESS,
161	            timestamp = now,
162	            metadata = null
163	        ))
164	
165	        return AppResult.Success(refund)
166	    }
167	}
